title: Replication Record
description: An independent replication of a prior claim. Replication is the strongest form of verification in this protocol. A claim that reaches 'replicated' status must have at least one replication record confirming the original result. A replication that fails to reproduce the result must record what was attempted and what diverged.

id.description: Unique identifier for this replication record. Use kebab-case. Example: peptide-x-ecoli-replication-1

claim_id.description: The id field of the claim this replication targets. Must exist in the relevant pack's claims.json.

replicator.description: The named replicator (GitHub username, agent identifier, or team name). Must differ from the original submitter of the claim. Self-replication is not replication.

replication_date.description: Date the replication was completed in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

method.description: The exact procedure used to replicate. A reader should be able to follow these steps and arrive at the same result (or explain the divergence). Include tool versions and any deviations from the original method.

runtime.description: Language runtime and version. Example: 'node 22.4.0' or 'python 3.12.3'

os.description: Operating system or CI runner. Example: 'ubuntu-24.04' or 'macos-15'

packages.description: Key package versions that affect the result. Example: 'ajv 8.20.0, prettier 3.8.3'

notes.description: Any environment detail that could affect reproducibility. Example: 'CI runner with 4 vCPU and 16 GB RAM'

environment.description: The execution environment. Stating it lets a future replicator detect whether a divergence is environmental rather than methodological.

input_hash.description: A hash (sha256 or equivalent) of the input data or source files used. If the input differs from the original submission, explain in the divergence field. A null hash means inputs are identical to the original — record that explicitly.

divergence.description: How the replicated result differs from the original. If the result is 'confirmed', state 'No divergence detected.' If the result differs, be specific about what changed and by how much. A replication with no divergence statement is incomplete.

confidence.description: Replicator confidence in their own replication. Not confidence in the original claim.
